content.problemStatement:
**Definitions:**

A string TTT is called **semi-palindrome** if you can rearrange the characters of TTT to make it into a palindrome.

For eg. if T=aabbT = aabbT=aabb, it isn't a palindrome as of now, but it can be rearranged to form abbaabbaabba, which is a palindrome. Thus, T=aabbT = aabbT=aabb is a semi-palindrome.

An **anti-palindrome** is the opposite of a semi-palindrome. In particular, a string TTT is called an anti-palindrome if it is not possible to rearrange the characters of TTT to make it into a palindrome.

For eg. if T=abcT = abcT=abc, there is no rearrangement of this string which makes it into a palindrome. Hence, T=abcT = abcT=abc is an anti-palindrome.

Now on to the problem:

You are given a string S=S1S2…SNS = S_1S_2 \ldots S_NS=S1​S2​…SN​ consisting of NNN english lowercase letters.

Your aim is to convert SSS into an anti-palindrome. For this, you are allowed to do the following operation as many times as you want (even 0 times) :


Select an index iii (1≤i≤N)(1 \leq i \leq N)(1≤i≤N) and change SiS_iSi​ to any other english lowercase letter.
Find the minimum number of operations needed to make SSS into an anti-palindrome.
constraints: The first line of input will contain a single integer CCC, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the length of the string SSS.
The next line contains the string SSS of length NNN.
For each test case, output the minimum number of operations needed to make SSS into an anti-palindrome.
SSS contains only english lowercase letters.
The sum of NNN over all testcases won't exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Testcase 1: The given string ababab is already an anti-palindrome, since there is no way to rearrange the letters to make it into a palindrome. So, we don't need to do any operations on it to make it an anti-palindrome. Hence the answer is 000.
Testcase 2: The given string aaaaaa is a palindrome, and so it is not an anti-palindrome. We can change it to ababab using 111 operation, and it becomes an anti-palindrome. Hence the answer is 111.
Testcase 3: The given string abcabcabc is already an anti-palindrome, since there is no way to rearrange the letters to make it into a palindrome. So, we don't need to do any operations on it to make it an anti-palindrome. Hence the answer is 000.
Testcase 4: The given string aaaaaaaaa is a palindrome, and so it is not an anti-palindrome. We can change it to abcabcabc using 222 operations, and it becomes an anti-palindrome. There is no way to make it into an anti-palindrome using only 1 operation. Hence the answer is 222.
Testcase 5: The given string abbabbabb is a semi-palindrome, since it can be rearranged to form babbabbab which is a palindrome. So abbabbabb is not an anti-palindrome. We can change it to abcabcabc using 111 operation, and it becomes an anti-palindrome. Hence the answer is 111.
